title: breaks
text: Interactive watershed break point delineation. golem-based Shiny app clicking stream networks place break points, delineating upstream watersheds, exporting sub-basins via pairwise subtraction.

title: breaks
text: Repository: NewGraphEnvironment/breaks Primary Language: R (golem Shiny package) Entry Point: app.R (golem launcher via pkgload::load_all() + breaks::run_app())

title: breaks
text: breaks sibling app NewGraphEnvironment family: fresh — FWA-referenced spatial hydrology (data layer — DB queries go fresh) flooded — floodplain delineation (generates optional AOI overlays) drift — land cover change (downstream consumer exported subbasins) diggs — golem Shiny app pattern follow (BC Historic Airphoto Explorer) fly — estimate airphoto footprints select optimal coverage study area Pipeline: fresh (network data) → breaks (delineate sub-basins) → flooded (delineate floodplains) → drift (classify land cover)

title: breaks
code: R/   run_app.R          — exported run_app()   app_ui.R           — bslib page_sidebar   app_server.R       — wires modules   app_config.R       — app_sys() and get_golem_config() helpers   breaks-package.R   — package-level doc and imports   mod_aoi.R          — AOI selection (3 methods: watershed group dropdown, click-to-delineate, upload gpkg/geojson)   mod_map.R          — leaflet map, click handling, stream display   mod_breaks.R       — break point management, snap, editable name_basin, compute via fresh::frs_watershed_split()   mod_export.R       — export controls (subbasins.gpkg + break_points.csv) data-raw/   example_neexdzii.R — worked example: Neexdzii Kwa AOI + floodplain overlay inst/   golem-config.yml   — golem config   app/www/           — static assets

title: breaks
text: Three methods set reactive aoi() polygon: 1. watershed_group_name — type-ahead dropdown querying whse_basemapping.fwa_watershed_groups_poly 2. Click--delineate — click basemap → snap via fresh::frs_point_snap() → delineate via fresh::frs_network() 3. Upload AOI — drag--drop gpkg/geojson

title: breaks
text: Streams fetched within AOI via fresh::frs_stream_fetch() User clicks streams place break points (uploads CSV lon/lat) point snapped via fresh::frs_point_snap() Editable name_basin column DT table — column editable Compute sub-basins via fresh::frs_watershed_split() (snap, delineate, sort, pairwise subtract, optional AOI clip) Export subbasins.gpkg + break_points.csv — name_basin extra columns preserved

title: breaks
text: Single shared connection created via fresh::frs_db_conn() app_server, passed conn modules, disconnected session end. DB access uses PG_*_SHARE env vars. hand-rolled SQL breaks — queries go fresh’s conn-first API.

title: breaks
text: use Claude Code agent teams vs subagents, key constraints. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-teams

title: breaks
text: Use agent teams teammates need talk — research debates, competing hypotheses, cross-layer coordination. Use subagents just need focused workers report back results. Good fit: parallel code review, investigating competing bug hypotheses, new modules don’t share files, research multiple angles. Bad fit: sequential tasks, edits file, simple work coordination overhead exceeds benefit.

title: breaks
text: Give enough context spawn prompt — teammates don’t inherit lead’s conversation history Ensure teammates different files — two teammates editing file leads overwrites Shut teammates cleanup — cleanup fails teammates still running Always clean via lead — teammates never run cleanup session resumption — /resume, spawn new teammates

text: Standards bookdown report projects across New Graph Environment.

title: breaks
text: canonical references. Child repos inherit structure patterns. mybookdown-template — General-purpose bookdown starter fish_passage_template_reporting — Fish passage reporting template doubt, match template . template production repos disagree, production wins — update template.

title: breaks
code: project/ ├── index.Rmd                # Master config, YAML params, setup chunks ├── _bookdown.yml            # book_filename, output_dir: "docs" ├── _output.yml              # Gitbook, pagedown, pdf_book config ├── 0100-intro.Rmd           # Chapter numbering: 4-digit, 100s increment ├── 0200-background.Rmd ├── 0300-methods.Rmd ├── 0400-results.Rmd ├── 0500-*.Rmd               # Discussion/recommendations ├── 0800-appendix-*.Rmd      # Appendices (site-specific in fish passage) ├── 2000-references.Rmd      # Auto-generated from .bib ├── 2090-report-change-log.Rmd  # Auto-generated from NEWS.md ├── 2100-session-info.Rmd    # Reproducibility ├── NEWS.md                  # Changelog (semantic versioning) ├── scripts/ │   ├── packages.R           # Package loading (renv-managed) │   ├── functions.R          # Project-specific functions │   ├── staticimports.R      # Auto-generated from staticimports pkg │   ├── setup_docs.R         # Build helper │   └── run.R                # Local build (gitbook + PDF) ├── fig/                     # Figures (organized by chapter or type) ├── data/                    # Project data ├── docs/                    # Rendered output (GitHub Pages) ├── renv.lock                # Locked dependencies └── .Rprofile                # Activates renv

title: breaks
text: Every index.Rmd follows setup sequence. Order matters. Responsive settings output format:
code: # 1. Gitbook vs PDF switch gitbook_on <- TRUE  # 2. Knitr options knitr::opts_chunk$set(   echo = identical(gitbook_on, TRUE),  # Show code only in gitbook   message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,   dpi = 60, out.width = "100%" ) options(scipen = 999) options(knitr.kable.NA = '--') options(knitr.kable.NAN = '--')  # 3. Source in order: packages → static imports → functions → data source('scripts/packages.R') source('scripts/staticimports.R') source('scripts/functions.R') # Gitbook photo_width <- "100%"; font_set <- 11  # PDF (paged.js) photo_width <- "80%"; font_set <- 9

title: breaks
text: Parameters live index.Rmd frontmatter (separate file). Child repos override editing values. Fish passage repos add project-specific params (project_region, model_species, wsg_code, update flags forms). project-specific — don’t add general template.
code: params:   repo_url: 'https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/repo_name'   report_url: 'https://www.newgraphenvironment.com/repo_name/'   update_packages: FALSE   update_bib: TRUE   gitbook_on: TRUE

title: breaks
text: Embed context purpose chunk names. principle universal; codes project-specific. Pattern: {type}-{system}-{description}

title: breaks
text: Bookdown auto-prepends fig: tab: chunk names. Tables: Table \@ref(tab:chunk-name) Figures: Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name) fig: tab: prefix chunk label — bookdown adds .

title: breaks
text: Interactive tables (DT) can’t use standard bookdown captions. Use my_tab_caption() function staticimports.R. Pattern: Separate -cap chunk table chunk. my_tab_caption() auto-grabs chunk label via knitr::opts_current$get()$label wraps HTML caption tags bookdown can cross-reference.
code: # Caption chunk — must use results="asis" {r tab-sites-sum-cap, results="asis"} my_caption <- "Summary of fish passage assessment procedures." my_tab_caption() # Table chunk — renders the DT {r tab-sites-sum} data |> my_dt_table(page_length = 20, cols_freeze_left = 0)

title: breaks
text: Separate prep chunk (find file) display chunk (render ).
code: # Prep — find the photo {r photo-196332-01-prep} my_photo1 <- fpr::fpr_photo_pull_by_str(str_to_pull = 'ds_typical_1_') my_caption1 <- paste0('Typical habitat downstream of PSCIS crossing ', my_site, '.') # Gitbook — full width {r photo-196332-01, fig.cap=my_caption1, out.width=photo_width, eval=gitbook_on} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) # PDF — side by side with 1% spacer {r photo-196332-d01, fig.show="hold", out.width=c("49.5%","1%","49.5%"), eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)} knitr::include_graphics(my_photo1) knitr::include_graphics("fig/pixel.png") knitr::include_graphics(my_photo2)

title: breaks
text: references.bib auto-generated — never edit manually. build, rbbt::bbt_write_bib() scans .Rmd files @citekey references, pulls BibTeX Zotero’s Better BibTeX, overwrites references.bib. manual additions lost next build. add reference: add shared Zotero group library, use BBT citation key (@key) .Rmd text, build. rbbt handles rest. update_bib: FALSE params, build uses existing references.bib without regenerating — useful offline builds CI Zotero isn’t running. Auto-generate package citations: Use nocite: YAML include references cited text.
code: bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" biblio-style: apalike link-citations: no knitr::write_bib(c(.packages(), 'bookdown', 'knitr', 'rmarkdown'), 'packages.bib')

Colonial acknowledgement (template-level): > Modern civilization long journey ahead acknowledge address historic ongoing impacts colonialism… Territorial acknowledgement (project-specific, must edited per report): Name Nations, governance systems, watersheds, species relevant project. use generic office-location acknowledgement — tie territory work happens. See Wedzin Kwa chinook example pattern. Funding partners (project-specific).

text: use # heading disclosure — creates separate chapter page gitbook. Instead, add YAML date: field renders title block: Wording principle: accurate LLM . assisted drafting synthesis — make scientific interpretations conclusions. say “independently verified authors” (redundant) attribute “ecological assessments” LLM. regulatory/EGBC-stamped work, use extended disclaimer soul/research/20260212_ai_disclosure_research.md. See NewGraphEnvironment/mybookdown-template#89.

title: breaks
text: single boolean gitbook_on controls output format throughout.
code: # Show only in gitbook {r map-interactive, eval=gitbook_on}  # Show only in PDF {r fig-print-only, eval=identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)}  # Conditional inline content `r if(identical(gitbook_on, FALSE)) knitr::asis_output("This report is available online...")`  # Page breaks for PDF only `r if(gitbook_on){knitr::asis_output("")} else knitr::asis_output("\\pagebreak")`

title: breaks
text: Reports use MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH versioning NEWS.md changelog. Version index.Rmd YAML: NEWS.md format: Auto-append appendix via my_news_to_appendix() staticimports.R: Convention: - Bump version index.Rmd add NEWS entry every commit main changes report content - Tag releases: git tag -v1.1.0 -m "v1.1.0: Brief description" - MAJOR: structural changes, new chapters, methodology changes - MINOR: new content, figures, tables, discussion sections - PATCH: prose fixes, corrections, formatting
code: date: |  |  | Version 1.1.0 DRAFT `r format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")` ## 1.1.0 (2026-02-17)  - Add feature X - Fix issue Y ([Issue #N](https://github.com/Org/repo/issues/N)) news_to_appendix(md_name = "NEWS.md", rmd_name = "2090-report-change-log.Rmd")

title: breaks
text: Always use ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog() — never hardcode viewer iframes. function includes cache-busting ?v= parameter. Bump v function default viewer.html breaking changes.
code: knitr::asis_output(ngr::ngr_str_viewer_cog("https://bucket.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/ortho.tif"))

title: breaks
text: Use renv reproducible package management: - .Rprofile activates renv startup - renv::restore() installs lockfile - renv::snapshot() updates lockfile adding packages - Use pak::pak("pkg") install (install.packages)

title: breaks
text: Production repos (2024-2025) drifted templates areas. working child repo, match repo , template: Script naming 02_reporting/ — older repos use tables.R, 0165-read-sqlite.R; newer repos use numbered 0130-tables.R. Follow repo ’re . Removed packages — elevatr, rayshader, arrow removed production still template. staticimports::import() call — repos skip source staticimports.R directly. Hardcoded vs parameterized years — older repos hardcode years file paths; newer repos use params$project_year. Prefer parameterized.

text: Use gq package shared layer symbology. Never hardcode hex color values registry style exists. Core pattern: reg$layers$lake, reg$layers$road, reg$layers$bec_zone, etc.
code: library(gq) reg <- gq_reg_main()  # load once per script — 51+ layers

text: project-specific layers main registry, use hand-curated CSV merge: Install: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/gq")
code: reg <- gq_reg_merge(gq_reg_main(), gq_reg_read_csv("path/to/custom.csv"))

title: breaks
text: sf_use_s2(FALSE) top every mapping script Compute area simplify SQL map title — title belongs report caption Legend least-important terrain — swap legend logo sides reduces AOI occlusion. fixed convention side. Four-corner rule — legend, logo, scale bar, keymap get corner. Never stack two quadrant. Bbox must match canvas aspect ratio — compute ratio geographic extents page dimensions. Mismatch causes white space bands. Consistent element--frame spacing — inset elements visually equal margins frame edge Map fills frame — basemap extends edge--edge, dead bands. Use near-zero inner.margins outer.margins. Suppress auto-legends — build manual ones registry values CAPS labels appear larger — use title case legend labels (gq gq_tmap_classes() handles automatically via to_title() fallback)

title: breaks
text: Read PNG check showing anyone: Correct polygon/study area shown? (verify source data, just bbox) Map fills page? (white/black bands) Keymap inside frame spacing edge? element overlap? (corner) Legend least-important terrain? Consistent spacing across elements? Scale bar breaks appropriate extent? See cartography skill full reference: basemap blending, BC spatial data queries, label hierarchy, mapgl gotchas, worked examples.

title: breaks
text: Use drift flooded together riparian land cover change analysis. flooded delineates floodplain extents DEMs stream networks; drift tracks ’s changing inside time. Pipeline: Class colors come drift’s shipped class tables (IO LULC, ESA WorldCover) production COGs S3, dft_map_interactive() serves tiles via titiler — set options(drift.titiler_url = "...") See drift vignette worked example (Neexdzii Kwa floodplain, 2017-2023)
code: # 1. Delineate floodplain AOI (flooded) valleys <- flooded::fl_valley_confine(dem, streams)  # 2. Fetch, classify, summarize (drift) rasters   <- drift::dft_stac_fetch(aoi, source = "io-lulc", years = c(2017, 2020, 2023)) classified <- drift::dft_rast_classify(rasters, source = "io-lulc") summary    <- drift::dft_rast_summarize(classified, unit = "ha")  # 3. Interactive map with layer toggle drift::dft_map_interactive(classified, aoi = aoi)

text: Structured checklist reviewing diffs commit. Used /code-check. Add new checks bug class discovered — compound time.

title: breaks
text: Variables double-quoted strings containing single quotes break value ' "echo '${VAR}'" — VAR contains ', shell syntax breaks Use printf '%s\n' "$VAR" | command pipe values safely Heredocs: unquoted <<EOF expands variables locally, <<'EOF' — know need

title: breaks
text: Hardcoded absolute paths (/Users/airvine/...) break users Use REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/<relative>" && pwd)" moving scripts, verify ../ depth still resolves correctly Usage comments match actual script location

title: breaks
text: || true hides real errors — failure actually safe ignore? Empty variable destructive operation (rm, destroy) — add guard: [ -n "$VAR" ] || exit 1 grep returning empty silently — downstream commands get empty input

title: breaks
text: Secrets passed command-line args visible ps aux Use env files, stdin pipes, temp files chmod 600 instead

title: breaks
text: Must pure ASCII — em dashes, curly quotes, arrows cause silent parse failure Check : perl -ne 'print "$.: $_" /[^\x00-\x7F]/' file.yaml

title: breaks
text: Behavioral guidelines reduce common LLM coding mistakes. Merge project-specific instructions needed. Tradeoff: guidelines bias toward caution speed. trivial tasks, use judgment.

title: breaks
text: Don’t assume. Don’t hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. implementing: - State assumptions explicitly. uncertain, ask. - multiple interpretations exist, present - don’t pick silently. - simpler approach exists, say . Push back warranted. - something unclear, stop. Name ’s confusing. Ask.

title: breaks
text: Minimum code solves problem. Nothing speculative. features beyond asked. abstractions single-use code. “flexibility” “configurability” wasn’t requested. error handling impossible scenarios. write 200 lines 50, rewrite . Ask : “senior engineer say overcomplicated?” yes, simplify.

title: breaks
text: Touch must. Clean mess. editing existing code: - Don’t “improve” adjacent code, comments, formatting. - Don’t refactor things aren’t broken. - Match existing style, even ’d differently. - notice unrelated dead code, mention - don’t delete . changes create orphans: - Remove imports/variables/functions changes made unused. - Don’t remove pre-existing dead code unless asked. test: Every changed line trace directly user’s request.

title: breaks
text: Define success criteria. Loop verified. Transform tasks verifiable goals: - “Add validation” → “Write tests invalid inputs, make pass” - “Fix bug” → “Write test reproduces , make pass” - “Refactor X” → “Ensure tests pass ” multi-step tasks, state brief plan: Strong success criteria let loop independently. Weak criteria (“make work”) require constant clarification. guidelines working : fewer unnecessary changes diffs, fewer rewrites due overcomplication, clarifying questions come implementation rather mistakes.
code: 1. [Step] → verify: [check] 2. [Step] → verify: [check] 3. [Step] → verify: [check]

title: breaks
text: Check duplicates: gh issue list --state open --search "<keywords>" – search creating Link SRED: work involves infrastructure, R&D, tooling, performance benchmarking, add Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N (match repo name SRED issue title) One issue, one concern. Keep focused.

title: breaks
text: Write issues clear technical focus: Use normal technical language titles descriptions Focus problem solution approach Add tracking links end (e.g., Relates Owner/repo#N) Issue body structure:
code: ## Problem <what's wrong or missing>  ## Proposed Solution <approach>  Relates to #<local> Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>

what: GitHub Issue Creation - Always Use Files
title: breaks
text: gh issue create command heredoc syntax fails repeatedly EOF errors. ALWAYS use --body-file:
code: cat > /tmp/issue_body.md << 'EOF' ## Problem ...  ## Proposed Solution ... EOF  gh issue create --title "Brief technical title" --body-file /tmp/issue_body.md

title: breaks
text: : Close issues via commit messages. commit closure documentation. DON’T: Close issues gh issue close. breaks audit trail — ’s linked diff showing changed. Fixes #N Closes #N — auto-closes links commit issue Relates #N — partial progress, close Always close issues work complete. Don’t leave stale open issues.
code: Fix broken DEM path in loading pipeline  Update hardcoded path to use config-driven resolution.  Fixes #20 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: breaks
text: Write clear, informative commit messages: commit: - Logical, atomic units work - Working state (tests pass) - Clear description changes avoid: - “WIP” “temp” commits main branch - Combining unrelated changes - Vague messages like “fixes” “updates”
code: Brief description (50 chars or less)  Detailed explanation of changes and impact.  Fixes #<issue> (or Relates to #<issue>) Relates to NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#<N>  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: breaks
text: Rules learned real project sessions. apply across repos. Install missing packages, don’t workaround — package needed, ask user install (e.g. pak::pak("pkg")). Don’t write degraded fallback code avoid dependency. Never hardcode extractable data — coordinates, station names, metadata can pulled API database runtime, . Don’t hardcode values programmatic source. Close issues via commits, gh issue close — see Closing Issues . Cite primary sources — see references conventions.

title: breaks
text: Pattern: noun_verb-detail – noun first, verb second across naming: Scripts logs live together: scripts/<module>/logs/

title: breaks
text: Projects = daily cross-repo tracking (always add relevant project) Milestones = iteration boundaries (release/claim prep) Don’t double-track unless ’s reason

title: breaks
text: Claude manages structured planning complex tasks using planning--files (PWF).

title: breaks
text: Use PWF task multiple phases, requires research, involves ~5 tool calls. Triggers: - User says “let’s plan ”, “plan mode”, “use planning”, invokes /planning-init - Complex issue work begins (multi-step, uncertain approach) - Claude judges task warrants structured tracking Skip planning single-file edits, quick fixes, tasks obvious next steps.

title: breaks
text: Explore first — Enter plan mode (read-). Read code, trace paths, understand problem proposing anything. task_plan.md — Phases checkbox tasks findings.md — Research, discoveries, technical analysis progress.md — Session log timestamps commit refs Commit plan — Commit planning files starting implementation. baseline. Work atomic commits — commit bundles code changes checkbox updates planning files. diff shows done checkbox marking done. Archive complete — Move planning/active/ planning/archive/ via /planning-archive.

what: Runnable examples on every exported function
title: breaks
text: Examples users discover function . must: - Actually run — \dontrun{} unless external resources required - Use bundled test data via system.file() work anyone - Show function useful — just runs, produces ’d use - Use qualified names non-exported dependencies (terra::rast(), sf::st_read()) since examples run user’s environment

title: breaks
text: least one vignette showing full pipeline real data: - Demonstrates package solving actual problem end--end - Uses bundled test data (committed inst/testdata/) - Hosted pkgdown users can read without installing Output format: Use bookdown::html_vignette2 (rmarkdown::html_vignette) figure numbering cross-references. Requires bookdown Suggests chunks must fig.cap numbered figures. Cross-reference Figure \@ref(fig:chunk-name). Vignettes need external resources (DB, API, STAC): use .Rmd.orig pre-knit pattern — breaks bookdown figure numbering knitr evaluates chunks pre-knit emits ![](path) markdown bookdown can’t number. Instead, separate data generation presentation: 1. data-raw/vignette_data.R — runs queries, saves results .rds inst/testdata/ (inst/vignette-data/) 2. Vignette loads .rds files, chunks run live pkgdown build 3. Note top vignette: “Data generated data-raw/script.R” 4. bookdown controls chunks — figure numbers, cross-refs work pattern test data: data-raw/ documents data produced, committed artifacts make vignettes reproducible without external resource.

title: breaks
text: Created via script data-raw/ documents exactly data produced (database queries, spatial crops, etc.) Committed inst/testdata/ — small enough ship package Used tests, examples, vignettes — one dataset, three purposes

title: breaks
text: roxygen2 exported functions @import @importFrom package-level doc (R/<pkg>-package.R) populate NAMESPACE — don’t rely :: everywhere function bodies pkgdown site public packages _pkgdown.yml (bootstrap 5) GitHub Action pkgdown (usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown"))

title: breaks
text: Run lintr::lint_package() committing R package code. Fix warnings — every lint worth fixing.

title: breaks
text: 120 char line length (default 80 strict data pipelines) Allow dotted.case (common base R legacy code) Suppress commented code lints (exploratory R scripts often commented alternatives) Exclude renv directory entirely
code: linters: linters_with_defaults(     line_length_linter(120),     object_name_linter(styles = c("snake_case", "dotted.case")),     commented_code_linter = NULL   ) exclusions: list(     "renv" = list(linters = "all")   )

title: breaks
text: Minimize Imports — use Suggests packages needed tests/vignettes Pin versions breaking changes known Prefer packages already tidyverse ecosystem

title: breaks
text: First release: one line (e.g., “Initial release. Brief description.”) Later releases: describe changed , function--function. Link pkgdown reference page details — don’t duplicate . Don’t list every function; pkgdown reference page single source truth ’s package. Bump version DESCRIPTION (e.g., 0.0.0.9000 → 0.1.0) Commit “Release vX.Y.Z” Tag: git tag vX.Y.Z && git push && git push --tags

title: breaks
text: Protect main deletion force pushes:
code: gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/rulesets --method POST --input - <<'EOF' {   "name": "Protect main",   "target": "branch",   "enforcement": "active",   "bypass_actors": [     { "actor_id": 5, "actor_type": "RepositoryRole", "bypass_mode": "always" }   ],   "conditions": { "ref_name": { "include": ["refs/heads/main"], "exclude": [] } },   "rules": [ { "type": "deletion" }, { "type": "non_fast_forward" } ] } EOF

title: breaks
text: usethis::create_package(".") usethis::use_mit_license("New Graph Environment Ltd.") usethis::use_testthat(edition = 3) usethis::use_pkgdown() usethis::use_github_action("pkgdown") usethis::use_directory("dev") — reproducible setup script usethis::use_directory("data-raw") — data generation scripts Hex sticker via hexSticker (see data-raw/make_hexsticker.R) Set GitHub Pages serve gh-pages branch

title: breaks
text: Keep dev/dev.R file documents every setup step. idempotent — run interactively. reproducible recipe package scaffold.

title: breaks
text: Keep README lean: - Hex sticker, one-line description, install, example showing ’s useful - Link pkgdown vignette function reference — don’t duplicate - Don’t maintain function table — ’s just another thing keep updated pkgdown’s reference page single source truth

title: breaks
text: LLM assistant modifies R package code: 1. Run lintr::lint_package() — fix issues committing 2. Run devtools::test() error grep — ensure tests pass one call: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::test()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(FAIL|ERROR|PASS)" | tail -5 3. Run devtools::document() grep results: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::document()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(Writing|Updating|warning)" | tail -10 4. Check devtools::check() passes releases — capture results one call: bash    Rscript -e 'devtools::check()' 2>&1 | grep -E "(ERROR|WARNING|NOTE|errors|warnings|notes)" | tail -10

title: breaks
text: references flow Claude Code, Zotero, technical writing New Graph Environment.

title: breaks
text: Three tools, different purposes. Use right one. Citation keys vs item keys: Citation keys (like irvine2020ParsnipRiver) come Better BibTeX. Item keys (like K7WALMSY) native Zotero. MCP works item keys. /zotero-lookup bridges citation keys item data. BBT citation key storage: Feb 2025+, BBT stores citation keys citationKey field directly zotero.sqlite (via Zotero’s item data system), separate BBT database. old better-bibtex.sqlite better-bibtex.migrated files stale longer updated. Query citation keys : SELECT idv.value items JOIN itemData id .itemID = id.itemID JOIN itemDataValues idv id.valueID = idv.valueID JOIN fields f id.fieldID = f.fieldID f.fieldName = 'citationKey'.

title: breaks
text: research turns reference: - DOI available: Tell user — Zotero’s magic wand (DOI lookup) fastest path - ResearchGate link: Flag user manual check — programmatic fetch blocked (403), full text often - BC gov report: Search ACAT, .gov.bc.ca library, EIRS viewer - Paywalled: Note , move . Don’t waste time trying bypass.

title: breaks
text: Preferred order: 1. DOI magic wand Zotero UI (fastest, complete metadata) 2. Web API POST collections array (grey literature, local PDFs — targets collection directly, UI interaction needed) 3. saveItems via /zotero-api (batch creation structured data — requires UI collection selection) 4. JS console script group library (connector can’t target right collection) Collection targeting: saveItems drops items whatever collection selected Zotero’s UI. Always confirm user calling . Web API bypasses — include "collections": ["KEY"] POST body. Find collection keys ?q=name search collections endpoint.

title: breaks
text: saveItems attachments silently fail. Don’t use . Instead: Web API S3 upload (preferred): Create attachment item → get upload auth → build S3 body (Python: prefix + file bytes + suffix) → POST S3 → register uploadKey. Works without Zotero running. See /zotero-api skill section 4. JS console fallback: Download curl, attach via item_attach_pdf.js Zotero JS console. Verify attachment exists via MCP: zotero_get_item_children

title: breaks
text: manual adds, confirm via MCP: - zotero_search_items — find title - zotero_get_item_metadata — check fields complete - zotero_get_item_children — confirm PDF attached

title: breaks
text: duplicates created (common saveItems retries): - Run collection_dedup.js via Zotero JS console - keeps copy attachments, trashes rest

title: breaks
text: rbbt pulls BBT, syncs Zotero. Edit references Zotero → rebuild report → bibliography updates. Library targeting: rbbt must know Zotero library search. set globally ~/.Rprofile: Without option, rbbt searches personal library (libraryID 1) won’t find group library references. library IDs map Zotero’s internal numbering — use /zotero-lookup SELECT DISTINCT libraryID citationkey BBT database discover available libraries.
code: # index.Rmd — dynamic bib from Zotero via Better BibTeX bibliography: "`r rbbt::bbt_write_bib('references.bib', overwrite = TRUE)`" # default library — NewGraphEnvironment group (libraryID 9, group 4733734) options(rbbt.default.library_id = 9)

title: breaks
text: [@key2020] — parenthetical: (Author 2020) @key2020 — narrative: Author (2020) [@key1; @key2] — multiple nocite: YAML — include uncited references

title: breaks
text: review paper references older study, trace back original cite . Don’t attribute findings review original exists. (See LLM Agent Conventions newgraph.md.) original unavailable (paywalled, print, can’t locate): use secondary citation format prose include bib entries sources: Smith et al. (2003; cited Doctor 2022) found … @smith2003 @doctor2022 go .bib file. reader can track original . Flag incomplete metadata primary entry — ’s better partial reference none .

title: breaks
text: need PDF obvious URL doesn’t work: DOI resolver → publisher site (often OA link) Europe PMC (europepmc.org/backend/ptpmcrender.fcgi?accid=PMC{ID}&blobtype=pdf) — ncbi blocks curl SciELO — needs User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 header ResearchGate — flag user manual download Semantic Scholar — sometimes OA links Ask user institutional access Always verify downloads: file paper.pdf say “PDF document”, HTML.

title: breaks
text: scripts/rag_build.R — maps citation keys Zotero PDF attachment keys, builds DuckDB data/rag/ gitignored — store local, committed Dependencies: ragnar, Ollama nomic-embed-text model See /lit-search skill full recipe

title: breaks
text: ragnar_store_connect() ragnar_retrieve() — returns chunks source file attribution.

title: breaks
text: NEVER write abstracts manually — CrossRef abstract, leave blank NEVER cite specific numbers without verifying source PDF via ragnar search NEVER paraphrase equations — copy exact notation cite page/section

title: breaks
text: SR&ED tracking integrates New Graph Environment’s development workflows.

title: breaks
text: SRED-eligible work across NGE falls single continuous project: Dynamic GIS-based Data Processing Reporting Framework Field: Software Engineering (2.02.09) Start date: May 2022 Fiscal year: May 1 – April 30 Consultant: Boast Capital (prepares final technical report) fragment work separate claims. fiscal year’s work structured iterations within one project. Internal tracking (experiment numbers sred-2025-2026) maps iterations — Boast assembles final narrative.

title: breaks
text: Tag hours sred_ref field linking relevant sred-2025-2026 issue number.

title: breaks
text: Link SRED-eligible issues tracking repo: Relates NewGraphEnvironment/sred-2025-2026#N

title: breaks
text: Eligible (systematic investigation overcome technological uncertainty): - Building tools/functions don’t exist standard practice - Prototyping new integrations systems (GIS ↔︎ reporting ↔︎ field collection) - Testing whether approach works documenting /didn’t - Iterating failed approaches new hypotheses eligible: - Standard configuration known tools - Routine bug fixes working systems - Writing reports using framework (’s service delivery) test: “try something weren’t sure work, learn something attempt?” yes, ’s likely eligible.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Irvine (2026). breaks: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation. R package version 0.3.0, https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/breaks.
code: @Manual{,   title = {breaks: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation},   author = {Al Irvine},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/breaks}, }

title: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation
text: Interactive watershed break point delineation — Shiny app clicking stream networks place break points, delineating upstream watersheds, exporting sub-basins via pairwise subtraction. Given stream network area interest, breaks lets place points streams, snap FWA network, delineate upstream watershed , compute non-overlapping sub-basins subtracting upstream catchments downstream ones. Upload CSV extra columns — ’re preserved snap export.

title: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/breaks")

title: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation
code: # Requires PostgreSQL with fwapg (via fresh) # Set PG_*_SHARE env vars for database connection breaks::run_app()

title: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation
text: Define AOI — three methods: Type-ahead dropdown BC watershed groups Click map delineate upstream watershed Upload GeoPackage GeoJSON Draw polygon map Place break points — click streams place points (upload CSV lon/lat columns). point snaps nearest FWA stream segment. Edit name_basin column table label sub-basin. Compute sub-basins — via fresh::frs_watershed_split(): delineate, sort area, pairwise subtract, optionally clip AOI. Export — download break_points.csv (columns preserved including name_basin) subbasins.gpkg (EPSG:3005).

title: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation
text: PostgreSQL fwapg (accessed via fresh) SSH tunnel direct connection database PG_*_SHARE environment variables configured

title: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation
text: breaks one piece larger watershed analysis workflow: Pipeline: fresh (network data) → breaks (sub-basins) → flooded (floodplains) → drift (land cover change).

title: breaks: Interactive Watershed Break Point Delineation — breaks-package
text: Interactive Shiny app clicking stream networks place break points, delineating upstream watersheds, exporting sub-basins via pairwise subtraction. Built fresh package FWA-referenced spatial hydrology queries.

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: Run Shiny Application

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
code: run_app(   onStart = NULL,   options = list(),   enableBookmarking = NULL,   uiPattern = "/",   ... )

title: Run the Shiny Application — run_app
text: onStart function called app actually run. needed shinyAppObj, since shinyAppDir case, global.R file can used purpose. options Named options passed runApp call (can following: "port", "launch.browser", "host", "quiet", "display.mode" "test.mode"). can also specify width height parameters provide hint embedding environment ideal height/width app. enableBookmarking Can one "url", "server", "disable". default value, NULL, respect setting previous calls  enableBookmarking(). See enableBookmarking() information bookmarking app. uiPattern regular expression applied GET request determine whether ui used handle request. Note entire request path must match regular expression order match considered successful. ... Arguments pass golem_opts. See `?golem::get_golem_options` details.

title: breaks 0.3.0
text: Add editable name_basin column break points table (#5) Migrate sub-basin computation fresh::frs_watershed_split() (#6) Pass name_basin sub-basin output map popups Sub-basin popups now show area basin name Remove internal watershed cache (fresh handles delineation) Migrate fresh conn-first API — single shared DB connection per session (#10) Fix download buttons working inside bslib sidebar

text: Rename package break breaks (break reserved word R) Update URLs references repo rename Fix CSV upload overwriting snap results stale values previous exports

title: breaks 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Interactive Shiny app watershed break point delineation BC’s FWA stream network.
